- Support for federated AWS role assumption from Okta (we prefer role chaining so we can require MFA at the IdP).
- SCIM provisioning for users + groups (group membership should drive RBAC roles on the control plane).
- A canonical mapping of Okta groups -> Redwood control-plane roles for audit.
- Short-lived service credentials and clear rotation guidance for any service accounts.

Date: 2026-11-28T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Billing units for long-context workflows

Hi Camila,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — our engineering team has one outstanding question before we can move to procurement: 

- How does Redwood account tokens for hybrid calls where we send a 150k-character context (approx. 40k tokens) and then stream short completions? Specifically: do you round up per-call to the next 1k token increment, or do you charge exact tokens with a monthly minimum? Our infra team is worried about unexpectedly large bills if every long-context call gets rounded.

Also, can we get a short written example of how rounding/minimums would apply to a weekly batch of ~2,000 such calls (peak/avg patterns included)? If you have a sample invoice or workbook that shows the math that would help.

Jordan ...

Cc: ops@brightleaf.com
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800

Hi Rafael,

We started seeing surges of 502/504s on our realtime websocket connections to Redwood around 07:30 PST th...
text:
From: Megan Lowe <megan@brightleaf.com>
To: rafael.mendes@redwood.ai
Cc: ops@brightleaf.com
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Production realtime feed intermittent timeouts (BrightLeaf)

Hi Rafael,

We started seeing surges of 502/504s on our realtime websocket connections to Redwood around 07:30 PST this morning. The symptom looks like clients receiving partial chunks then a connection close; retries spike downstream. Impact: our realtime dashboards fall behind (up to ~30s) and our customer-facing chat has degraded — error rate up ~12% on the streaming API and p50->p99 latency has widened ~3x.

We attached a small capture of the connection timeline and a short screenshot of the 503s. Can you route this to the on-call engineering and advise on mitigation? This is affecting our Monday morning traffic and the CTO is asking for an update.

Attachments:
- conn-timeline-11-25....

Hi Rafael —

We ran the monthly remittance reconciliation and found a ~22% shortfall on the Oct GCP Marketplace payout for Apex Analytics (account: APEX-001). The payout we received on 2026-11-01 is materially lower than the invoice/usage records we have.

Quick summary:
- Expected settlement gross: USD 1,240,832 (per invoice bundle attached)
- Amount paid: USD 967,240
- Difference: USD 273,592

We suspect either: a) marketplace withholding due to entity mapping (APEX EMEA vs APEX APAC), or b) a subset of metered SKUs were reclassified and priced differently at settlement.

Date: 2026-07-12T02:10:00-07:00
Subject: Immediate maintenance: urgent scheduler patch affecting inference latency

Hi Sam —

We observed a sharp uptick in tail latency for short-sequence generation (p95/p99) affecting several Acme endpoints starting ~2026-07-12T01:40Z. Root cause appears to be a scheduler regression in the runtime that increases small-batch scheduling contention under certain traffic patterns.

We’re preparing a hotfix that addresses a race in the scheduler's ready queue which should restore normal latency characteristics. We plan a short, rolling maintenance window starting 2026-07-12T03:30Z UTC; each pool will be patched and health-checked (expected per-pool downtime ~30–60s).

What we...

Attached is a small pcap capture (tcpdump -w) and a traceroute from one of our application nodes. Kernel logs around the time of the issue are in dmesg-snippet-07jul.txt. We haven't changed app config — only routed traffic via the new connector and applied the SG changes per the cutover doc.

Subject: Question on paying VAL-3921 (GBP invoice) — FX, intermediary fees & VAT

Hi Neha,

We received Invoice VAL-3921 (GBP) and intend to pay from our EUR account. Before we proceed, a couple of quick clarifications:

1) Currency handling: Do you accept EUR remittances and will you convert on your side, or should we send GBP to your UK collection account? If you accept EUR, can you confirm the exact IBAN/beneficiary details and any reference we must include?

2) Intermediary bank fees: Our bank typically uses SHA by default and intermediaries sometimes deduct a portion (we've seen £15–£45 taken on similar amounts). Will Redwood accept a net-of-fees payment or do you require OUR (sender pays) so the invoice ...
